content: A generating series is a formal power series whose coefficients encode information about a sequence. It is defined as $G(x) = \sum_{n=0}^{\infty} g_n x^n$, where $g_n$ is the $n$th term of the sequence. Generating series can be used to solve counting problems by encoding the answers as power series and then extracting the desired information from the series. They can also be used to find closed-form expressions for sequences and to solve recurrence relations.

In this chapter and the next we will see how to use this strategy to encode the answers to various counting problems as generating series. In Chapter 4 we will see how to get numbers out of these power series in order to answer the counting problems explicitly.
